problem.title: Two Sum
problem.description: Given an array of integers nums and an integer target, return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to target.
assumptions: You may assume that each input would have exactly one solution
You may not use the same element twice
You can return the answer in any order

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Brute Force
solutions.description: The simplest approach is to check every pair of numbers in the array.

solutions.title: Solution 2 – Hash Map (Optimal)
solutions.description: Use a hash map to store complements. For each number, check if its complement (target - num) exists in the map.

solutions.title: Solution 3 – Two Pointers (If Array is Sorted)
solutions.description: If the array is sorted, we can use two pointers from both ends to find the pair in linear time.

complexity.time: O(n log n) if sorting needed, O(n) if already sorted

variations.title: Two Sum II - Input Array Is Sorted
variations.description: Same problem but array is sorted in ascending order. Use two pointers approach.

variations.title: Two Sum III - Data Structure Design
variations.description: Design a data structure that supports add and find operations:
